channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: WarnerBros. Discovery announces major corporate restructure
item.description: Media conglomerate WarnerBros. Discovery (WBD) announced a major restructuring of its business on Thursday, splitting its linear and streaming arms into two separate companies as it eyes potential mergers and acquisitions in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — Top U.S. officials were in the Middle East on Thursday, pushing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in a few weeks.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Francisco names street for Associated Press photographer who captured the iconic Iwo Jima photo
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A photojournalist who captured one of the most enduring images of World War II — the U.S. Marines raising the flag on the Japanese island of Iwo Jima — will have a block in downtown San Francisco named for him Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Insurance company denies covering cost of life-saving medicine for woman
item.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City denied covering the cost of a metro woman’s medicine that could save her life.
item.dc:creator: Malik Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Woman threatens insurance company, says 'Delay, Deny, Depose': Police
item.description: Near the end of the call, investigators said Boston could be heard stating, "Delay, Deny, Depose. You people are next.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA chief Michael Whitaker to step down when Trump takes office
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration chief Mike Whitaker will step down when President-elect Donald Trump takes office, following Elon Musk's calls for Whitaker's resignation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year and rings the New York Stock Exchange's opening bell
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its person of the year.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: RFK Jr. wants daughter-in-law in CIA top role to probe JFK assassination
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. reportedly wants Amaryllis Fox Kennedy and Trump to help release documents about the JFK assassination.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump mass deportation threats infuriate immigrant advocates
item.description: President-elect Trump’s pledge to enact mass deportations and end birthright citizenship, potentially affecting millions of U.S. citizens, is raising alarm bells among immigrant communities who fear an overzealous Trump administration could destroy countless families.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Used this parking app? You may qualify for part of $32.8 million settlement
item.description: An estimated 21 million users of the ParkMobile parking app are said to have been impacted by the data breach.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells TIME he will pardon Jan. 6 rioters on day 1
item.description: The President-elect, who will serve his second term as the first American president to be convicted of a felony, was named TIME's 2024 Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rural towns push to divorce from big cities, form new conservative state
item.description: New move to secede from blue parts of California is being pushed by group hoping to created a new state away from the Democratic-controlled Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turning dusty attic treasures into cash can yield millions for some and disappointment for others
item.description: THOMASTON, Maine (AP) — Kaja Veilleux has been hunting New England attic treasures for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Family of UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect didn’t report him as police ask what they knew during manhunt
item.description: Of more than 200 tips sent to the NYPD regarding the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, none named suspect Luigi Mangione, who was arrested Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Southern California forecast of cool temps, calm winds to help firefighters battle Malibu blaze
item.description: Cooler temperatures, calmer winds and a chance of rainfall in Southern California this week are forecast to help firefighters as they battle a wind-driven wildfire.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, Jae C. Hong And Gabriela Aoun Angueira, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Dakota regulators consider underground carbon dioxide storage permits for Midwest pipeline
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A North Dakota panel will consider Thursday whether to approve permits for underground storage of hundreds of millions of metric tons of carbon dioxide that a proposed pipeline would carry from ethanol plants throughout the Midwest.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealth Group CEO encourages staff to make health care work better for everyone after fatal shooting
item.description: UnitedHealth Group CEO Andrew Witty encouraged colleagues to continue making health care work better for everybody after the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The man accused of killing the head of one of the biggest U.S. health insurance companies is fighting being sent to face charges in New York City, where the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes bill to make ‘flag planting’ in Ohio State stadium a felony
item.description: Days after the postgame melee between the Michigan and Ohio State football teams, an Ohio state lawmaker has introduced a new bill that would make “flag planting” within Ohio Stadium a felony criminal offense.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Santa’s home is on Zillow, and you can take an AI-powered tour
item.description: You can take an AI-powered tour of Santa’s home, and you can even decorate your home like Santa with these festive décor ideas.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FTC issuing refunds to Fortnite gamers after unwanted charges
item.description: According to the FTC, the company unlawfully charged players for unwanted purchases, let children rack up unauthorized charges without their parents’ permission, and blocked some users who disputed wrongful charges.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, ABC 4

item.title: Poet and literary celebrity Nikki Giovanni dies at 81
item.description: The poet, author, educator and public speaker who rose from borrowing money to release her first book to decades as a literary celebrity sharing her blunt and conversational takes on everything from racism and love to space travel and mortality, has died. She was 81.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A brushfire in Malibu prompts evacuations as it burns near Pepperdine University
item.description: Firefighters battled a brushfire early Tuesday in Malibu, near Pepperdine University, prompting evacuations amid dangerous fire conditions because of Southern California’s notorious Santa Ana winds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Miami's Ward, Oregon's Gabriel, Colorado's Hunter, Boise State’s Jeanty, are named Heisman finalists
item.description: Oregon's Dillon Gabriel, Colorado's Travis Hunter, Boise State’s Ashton Jeanty, and Miami's Cam Ward were announced as the Heisman Trophy finalists on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON AP College Football Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Auctioneer defends The Onion's bid for Infowars as Alex Jones tries stopping sale
item.description: An auction company executive involved in the liquidation of Alex Jones’ conspiracy theory platform Infowars testified for nearly five hours Monday, as a bankruptcy judge in Texas began a hearing into whether The Onion satirical news outlet was properly named the winning bidder over a company affilia
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden creates Native American boarding school national monument to mark era of forced assimilation
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President Joe Biden designated a national monument at a former Native American boarding school in Pennsylvania on Monday to honor the resilience of Indigenous tribes whose children were forced to attend the school and hundreds of similar abusive institutions.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Military pauses Osprey flights again after more metal failures are found in near crash in November
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon is temporarily pausing flights again of its fleet of V-22 Ospreys after weakened metal components possibly played a role in another near crash, the latest setback for an embattled aircraft whose safety problems have grown.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about Luigi Mangione, the suspect arrested in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5
